riskFactors: BENJI has limited verified secondary-market or DEX liquidity; DeFiLlama reports no DeFi active TVL/liquidity, so liquidity depends primarily on issuer redemption rather than open secondary markets.
The asset is permissioned and transfer-restricted, which is appropriate for compliance but limits free market transferability and secondary-market depth.
Multi-chain deployment across Stellar, Polygon, Arbitrum, Avalanche, Aptos, Ethereum, Base, and Solana increases operational monitoring and cross-chain infrastructure complexity.
Several non-EVM or secondary-chain deployments have isVerified false because source-code verification or equivalent explorer verification was not clearly represented in the normalized dataset.
No public legalOpinionUrl is included in the normalized compliance layer, although SEC registration and prospectus filings substantially reduce legal uncertainty.
On-chain proof-of-reserves oracle fields are null; reserve confidence comes from SEC filings, audited fund statements, and traditional fund reporting rather than cryptographic PoR.
mitigants: BENJI represents shares of a U.S.-registered government money market fund organized as a series of Franklin Templeton Trust and regulated under the SEC framework.
The fund invests at least 99.5% of total assets in U.S. government securities, cash, and repurchase agreements collateralized fully by U.S. government securities or cash.
The fund has audited financial/statement materials, with PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP identified as auditor and JPMorgan Chase Bank identified as custodian/sub-administrator in normalized data.
Franklin Templeton Investor Services, LLC acts as transfer agent and maintains the official record of share ownership through a blockchain-integrated system.
The fund has large scale and market presence, with approximately 824.62M USD AUM and 1,107 holders in the normalized dataset.
Regulatory structure is significantly stronger than offshore or exempt tokenized note products because the fund is registered and operates as a government money market fund under Rule 2a-7.
Issuer redemption via the Benji App or Institutional Web Portal provides a direct liquidity path even without verified DEX depth.
Compliance controls, KYC requirements, and permissioned transfer restrictions reduce regulatory and illicit-finance exposure.
